exercises.heading: Food and Restaurants
exercises.description: Person A {v} Fine dining is something that not everyone knows how to appreciate. A lot of hours of education goes into training people who work at these types of establishments, and those who attend high quality restaurants are expected to get great food and service. I don’t understand why one would want to have a meal at a low quality place with regular food when the option of a good meal is on the table. {v} Person B {v} Everyone should get the opportunity to experience fine dining, just so they know what it’s like. I can’t help but recognize the fact that it really is an art, but some people might feel a bit out of place in a situation like that. I personally enjoy it, but it’s just a financial investment that I can’t be doing all the time so I usually keep my fancy restaurant experiences to celebrate special occasions. {v} Person C {v} I’ve experienced everything from fast food chains to 5 star restaurants and I will always choose the first. When I’m hungry I want something that I can get without having to sit down, wait for and put a napkin on my lap to eat. I once considered working at a fancy restaurant, but I thought my time would be better spent learning another skill that I could use in a variety of places. At the end of the day, food is food, no matter how it’s served. {v} Person D {v} One of my dreams is to get all dressed up and go to one of these fine-dining restaurants where everyone is waiting on me and serving me small dishes with high-quality food. It shocks me how much work can be put into cooking, the presentation of the food and the presentation of the waiters and cooks! If I had the money I would definitely spend it on an experience like that - I think it would be something unforgettable in my life.
exercises.subtitle: Four people respond in the comments section of an online magazine article about food and restaurants. Read their comments and answer the questions. {v}
exercises.label: Reading Part 4

questions.title: 1. Who thinks that a good restaurant is always the best choice?
2. Who thinks that fine-dining should be considered an art?
3. Who thinks high-end restaurants are best for unique situations?
4. Who prefers to eat cheap food than going to a nice restaurant?
5. Who thinks that going to a fancy restaurant is too much work?
6. Who has never gone to a high quality restaurant?
7. Who is impressed by the service at nice restaurants?

exercises.description: Read the text below. Match the headings A-H to the paragraphs 19-25.
exercises.label: Reading Part 5

answers: C. How to do telepathic impression
E.Steps to take to become a mind reader
questions.title: 19. Telepathic impression is a transfer of thoughts from one mind to the other. The person {v} who is sending the thought, the sender, creates the impression. The person who is {v} receiving the thoughts, the receiver, accepts the impression from the sender. {v} Impressions are one of the most important things in telepathy. Writing down received {v} impressions or thoughts is encouraged, in order to remember them. Senders can verify {v} what the receiver has written down, to confirm that the thoughts match. In order to {v} send and receive impressions, both senders and receivers have to keep a clear mind. {v} Regular practice can improve people’s telepathic powers. It is important to be patient, {v} as one cannot master telepathic impression within a few tries.

answers: F. Mind Reading as a profession
questions.title: 20. Mind reading involves reading thoughts from a person’s mind without physical or {v} sensory interaction. It’s a form of telepathy. There are professional mind readers, who {v} give readings by observing people. Professionals in this field have been able to {v} develop entire shows that leave audience members and viewers shocked and amazed. {v} Though often simply a series of tricks and well-crafted situations, mind readers, or {v} mentalists as they’re also referred to, use the natural fascination surrounding this topic, {v} as well as their keen insight into the human mind and how it can be manipulated, to {v} create fantastic scenarios that truly give the appearance of mind reading.

answers: B. Is traditional mind reading a legitimate practice?
questions.title: 21. Although mind reading is popular, there are still plenty of skeptics. Many have doubts {v} about its accuracy and fears about what practical uses may be developed if it could {v} indeed be harnessed and used at will. Many in the scientific community say that the {v} validity of mind reading does not have any proven evidence. The practices of mind {v} reading or telepathy in the western world dates back to the 19th century. As of today, {v} mind reading is not believed possible through the limited use of our five senses. {v} However, modern mind reading is performed, to some degree, by using neuroimaging {v} methods.

22. Mental communication is the interaction of two different minds to exchange thoughts, {v} feelings, and ideas. It is a well-known form of telepathy. Many are concerned about how {v} others think of us. With the techniques of mental communication, it is possible to {v} know what another person is thinking at that time. It is also possible to know what a {v} {v} person is feeling. {v} It can be used as a way of helping people cope with difficulties. {v} Even without speaking a single word, mental communication is possible.

23. Many experts have tried to communicate with animals too. There are those that believe {v} telepathy is, in fact, the main form through which animals communicate to each other. {v} It’s easy to jump to these conclusions when clear signs of communication exist {v} between two or more animals, but often being done without the use of verbal language, {v} as humans are accustomed to in our interactions. Telepathic communication with {v} animals has never been a practice upheld by science, but it has rendered some curious {v} interactions with a range of animals, including dogs, cats, horses, dolphins, rats, and {v} so on.

answers: H. What’s involved in mind control?
questions.title: 24. Mind control is the process of influencing one’s thoughts; and therefore, their actions. It {v} is a form of telepathy that has been practised for many decades. Mind control is also {v} known by the terms ‘brainwashing’ and ‘thought control.’ There are both positive and {v} negative applications. Because of the potential use for unethical purposes, it’s not {v} widely practiced. However, there may also be use for it in the field of medicine. Methods {v} of mind control provoke the subject to take certain actions, and may also change {v} one’s thought patterns. It can be accomplished through the use of media, like television, {v} Internet, print and radio. Hypnosis has also been proven an effective method of mind {v} control.

25. The various methods of telepathy can prove to be beneficial in our day-to-day lives. {v} With telepathic powers, we can build new levels of communication. Often, people find it {v} hard to communicate with words alone. Sometimes words are not enough to express {v} what we are feeling. In such scenarios, telepathy can become quite useful. To {v} communicate with people who are deaf, blind and cannot speak, telepathic methods are {v} used. It can bring a new understanding about the mind of one or more individuals. {v} Telepathy can be used by medical professionals and law enforcement to make {v} advances in their particular fields. It can generate better communication between {v} married people, children, and also with mentally ill people.
